step_by_step_thinking: In acute myocardial infarction, cardiac enzymes such as creatine phosphokinase (CPK), lactate dehydrogenase (LDH), and aspartate transaminase (AST or SGOT) typically elevate. Among these, CPK is known for its rapid increase within hours after an MI due to its high concentration in cardiac muscle cells compared to other tissues. This makes it one of the earliest markers detectable post-infarct onset.
raw_response:
In acute myocardial infarction, cardiac enzymes such as creatine phosphokinase (CPK), lactate dehydrogenase (LDH), and aspartate transaminase (AST or SGOT) typically elevate. Among these, CPK is known for its rapid increase within hours after an MI due to its high concentration in cardiac muscle cells compared to other tissues. This makes it one of the earliest markers detectable post-infarct onset.

The correct answer is A: CPK. The rise in CPK levels can be observed quite early following an MI, often peaking around 48-72 hours later.
